user.title: Multiroom Climate
user.description: Regulate your home to an average of room sensors by wrapping an existing thermostat.

data_description.climate_entity: The thermostat this controller will drive (kept in its AUTO band).
data_description.target_sensors: The room sensors whose average is the temperature to hold.
data_description.humidity_sensor: Optional. When set, cooling overcools slightly while humidity is above target to wring out moisture.

reconfigure.title: Change sensors
reconfigure.description: Update the averaged room sensors or the humidity sensor. The wrapped thermostat can't be changed here — add a new entry to control a different one. Learned calibration is kept.

error.no_sensors: Select at least one target temperature sensor.

abort.already_configured: This thermostat is already controlled by a Multiroom Climate entry.

thermostat_missing.title: Multiroom Climate can't find its thermostat
thermostat_missing.description: The thermostat `{entity_id}` that Multiroom Climate controls is no longer available in Home Assistant. Restore that entity (check the integration that provides it), or reconfigure Multiroom Climate to control a different thermostat. While it's missing, the integration can't regulate and your thermostat keeps its own settings.

init.title: Day/night schedule
init.description: Optional setback schedule. When enabled, the target follows a day and a night setpoint, reached early by the optimal-start lead. Temperatures are in your system's unit.

data_description.optimal_start_lead: How early to begin each transition so the home reaches the new setpoint by its scheduled time.
